method: VK_QUERY_TYPE_TIMESTAMP around each vkCmdDispatch (TOP→BOTTOM of pipe), 3 warm-up + 9 measured submits, median µs. Synthetic zeroed inputs; correctness covered by vulkan_verify (8/8 PASS on Intel ARL). Spec-constant sweep over BLOCKS_PER_WG / TOKENS_PER_WG ∈ {1,2,4,8,16} (constant_id 0) for the _multi kernels; n_kv ∈ {512,4096,32768}.

host.loadAvgDuringNvidiaRun: ~30-40 (sibling agents); GPU timestamps measure GPU time so are load-insensitive

devices.verdict: bandwidth-bound at n_kv>=512; the _multi fold is a wash below 32k. Device-policy: fold factor 4, QJL engage threshold 1024 tokens, TBQ engage threshold 8192 tokens.

fused_attn_qjl_polar_us.note: 8 WGs total, cannot fill an Arc — unfused score+softmax+V is ~10x faster, stays default

devices.icd: proprietary nvidia driver 580.142 (nvidia_icd.json) — now exposes a Vulkan ICD with the kmod loaded
devices.verdict: the _multi fold is a win at EVERY measured length — discrete GPU, plenty of SMs to hide latency. Device-policy: TBQ fold factor 16, QJL fold factor 8; thresholds left at 1024/8192 (same as iGPU) so the small end keeps single-block.

fused_attn_qjl_polar_us.note: still 8 WGs — unfused path far faster here too; this is a future grid rewrite

specConstantDevicePolicy.note: AMD wave64 wants its own sweep

parity.vulkan-dispatch-smoke: 7/7 PASS against the existing linux-x64-vulkan fork build

remaining: Fork rebuild (linux-x64-vulkan) + end-to-end llama-bench on eliza-1-0_6b / 1_7b — deferred: box too contended and build-llama-cpp-dflash.mjs git-reset-hard's the fork submodule which has live CPU/CUDA build dirs. The shader sources + device-policy patches are committed; the next --target linux-x64-vulkan build picks them up, then make vulkan-dispatch-smoke for fresh-binary parity.
fused-attn (q_head, kv_tile) 2D grid rewrite — the 8-WG-per-head shape is the bottleneck on any GPU with >8 cores; not on the runtime hot path yet.
AMD wave64 sweep — the device-policy table has NVIDIA + Intel rows; AMD/Adreno/Mali still on the conservative default.
